title: Mode Bridge - Open App
meta_description: mode bridge opens the official app to deposit or withdraw supported assets between Ethereum and Mode Mainnet.
hero_h2: Move assets between Ethereum and Mode Mainnet
hero_lead: mode bridge opens the official route for deposits and withdrawals between Ethereum and Mode Mainnet using Mode's canonical bridge flow.
what_is: Mode is an OP Stack Layer 2 focused on DeFi and onchain agents; its canonical bridge moves supported assets between Ethereum Mainnet and Mode Mainnet. See the Mode bridge guide for the official deposit and withdrawal framing.

how.d: Use the official bridge domain listed in Mode network details, then connect a wallet you control.
Choose a deposit from Ethereum to Mode or a withdrawal from Mode back to Ethereum.
Pick a supported asset such as ETH, USDC, USDT, or WBTC and enter the amount.
Check the domain, network, token, amount, gas prompt, and destination before signing.

points.p: Mode docs describe the canonical bridge as the route for moving funds between Ethereum Mainnet and Mode Mainnet.
The user guide lists ETH, USDC, USDT, and WBTC as supported assets, so check the app before sending anything else.
Mode Mainnet uses ETH as its currency symbol and chain ID 34443; confirm against Mode network details before transacting.

faq.a: mode bridge is the canonical Mode flow for deposits and withdrawals between Ethereum Mainnet and Mode Mainnet.

faq.q: Is mode bridge the official app?
faq.a: Use the bridge domain listed in Mode network details and verify the URL before connecting a wallet.

faq.q: Which assets can I use on mode bridge?
faq.a: Mode docs list ETH, USDC, USDT, and WBTC as supported for the canonical bridge flow.
Yes. Mode docs use deposit for Ethereum to Mode and withdrawal for Mode back to Ethereum.

faq.q: What should I check before using mode bridge?
faq.a: Confirm the domain, wallet network, token, amount, destination chain, and wallet prompt before signing any transaction.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify the destination domain before connecting. Educational content only, not financial advice.
